changes.text: Simplified the footer into a matching card-style block that only shows rights reserved and creator credit.
Removed extra footer navigation and descriptive text to keep the bottom of the site clean.
Redesigned command dropdowns into cleaner accordion cards with clearer usage, availability, visibility, options and notes.
Improved the website footer with better structure, navigation links and creator credit.
Removed decorative emojis from the updated command documentation interface for a cleaner look.
Command documentation now expands directly under the selected command instead of appearing at the end of the commands area.
Added a cleaner accordion-style command interaction with open and close behavior.
Removed the awkward separate command details panel that made users scroll away from the selected command.
Added clickable command cards with detailed documentation for each slash command.
Command details now show usage, options, visibility, DM support and helpful notes.
Commands page is now prepared for deeper documentation and future Discord login visibility rules.
Added a public changelog section to track Meowz updates.
Added live slash command documentation with search and category filters.
Kept support and user-only sections hidden until Discord login is added.
Connected the website to the bot API for live stats.
Added live bot name, avatar, ping, servers, users, commands and uptime.
Website now updates bot stats automatically every 30 seconds.
Added Discord embed preview metadata and preview image.
Improved how the website appears when shared in Discord.
Launched the first Meowz website on Railway.
Added homepage, invite button, feature cards and bot branding.
